##NAMES=Delhayelite
##RRUFFID=R070096
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=K_7_Na_3_Ca_5_Al_2_Si_14_O_38_F_4_Cl_2_
##LOCALITY=Yukspor Mountain, Khibiny Massif, Kola Peninsula, Russia
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Dark gray to colorless cleavage fragments associated with brown lamprophyllite, elongated blue aegerine, light yellow fenakite, grayish brown lomonosovite and very pale yellow to colorless Fenaksite (R080135).
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R070096
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=K_7.25_Na_3.44_(Ca_4.32_Na_0.66_Mn_0.02_)(Al_1.97_Fe^3+^_0.03_)(Si_13.97_Al_0.03_)O_38_F_4_(Cl_1.70_F_0.30_)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
